questions.question: A model in physics must be 100% accurate to reality.
questions.explanation: No! Models are simplified versions. They're not perfect. They're good enough to understand and predict.

questions.question: What's the main purpose of using a model?
options: To make things simpler without losing important details
To hide what we don't understand
questions.explanation: Models simplify reality so we can understand and solve problems. They're simpler but still accurate enough to work.

questions.question: When a model stops working in a new situation, the model is bad.
questions.explanation: No! It just means you need a different model for that situation. Every model has a limit—that's expected.

questions.question: When might the 'ball as a point' model NOT work?
options: Calculating how far the ball travels
questions.explanation: If you need to know spin, size matters. The 'point' model ignores size, so it breaks for that.
